div.h2: Step-by-step: fork → setpgid → tcsetpgrp to job → wait → tcsetpgrp back to shell

ol.li: setpgid BEFORE exec: Must occur in child before execve(). Parent also calls it to ensure PGID exists for tcsetpgrp.
tcsetpgrp BEFORE wait: Shell must transfer terminal before calling waitpid to allow job to handle signals properly.
tcsetattr RESTORE: Shell MUST save and restore termios structs; interactive jobs often corrupt terminal modes (raw vs cooked).

div.p: Fail Case (Parent Only): Parent calls setpgid. Child calls exec. If exec finishes before setpgid, syscall fails (EACCES).
Fail Case (Child Only): Child calls setpgid. Parent calls tcsetpgrp. If parent wins, tcsetpgrp fails (child not in group yet).
Success Path: Both call. Atomic establishment guaranteed.
Requirement: BOTH parent AND child must call setpgid().

ul.li: If Parent calls first: Group exists when child starts.
If Child calls first: Group exists when parent tries to move child.
Failure to call in parent results in race where child execs before being moved.

div.p: Signal Handling Shell must ignore SIGTTOU (Signal 22) otherwise writing to TTY while changing ownership stops the shell.
Driver filtering: Input and signals (SIGINT/SIGQUIT) now directed ONLY to PGID ${child_pid}.
Shell Status Suspended execution. TTY belongs to job.
